text.title: Model Routing and Async Batching
text.summary: Rule-based model routing (cheap default with capable fallback), BatchProcessor async pattern, and OpenAI Batch API for 50% cost discount on offline pipelines.

rule.statement: Use rule-based routing (token count thresholds, task type keyword matching) — never use an LLM call to decide which model to call.
rule.rationale: Routing via another LLM call is circular (costs money, adds latency) and creates a dependency that can fail. Rule-based routing is deterministic, free, and instant.

rule.statement: After implementing model routing, evaluate quality on a held-out test set — model downgrade silently degrades quality without A/B evaluation.
rule.rationale: Routing to a cheaper model saves cost only if quality is acceptable; routing without evaluation trades hidden quality loss for visible cost savings.

antipattern.description: Using an LLM to classify prompt complexity and select the routing model.
antipattern.reason: The routing call itself costs money and adds 200-500ms latency; for tasks shorter than 500 tokens the routing overhead exceeds the savings.

rule.statement: Use AsyncOpenAI with asyncio.gather to parallelize multiple independent prompts; never call completions.create sequentially in a for loop for batch work.
rule.rationale: Sequential calls at 1s latency each make batch pipelines 10-100x slower than necessary; async gather reduces wall-clock time to the latency of a single call.

example.code:
import asyncio from openai import AsyncOpenAI
async def batch_complete(prompts: list, model: str = "gpt-4o-mini") -> list: client = AsyncOpenAI() tasks = [ client.chat.completions.create( model=model, messages=[{"role": "user", "content": p}], ) for p in prompts ] responses = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True) return [ r.choices[0].message.content if not isinstance(r, Exception) else f"Error: {r}" for r in responses ]
example.note: return_exceptions=True prevents one API error from aborting the entire batch.

rule.statement: Use OpenAI Batch API for any pipeline that tolerates 24-hour latency (nightly reports, bulk classification, offline annotation) — it provides a 50% cost discount.
rule.rationale: Batch API queues requests for async processing; the 50% discount is OpenAI's standard pricing for offline workloads that do not require real-time response.

text.reference: Prompt for classifying call sites as cacheable/downgradeable/batch-eligible
